2010-01-01.title: Video of Attack on Protester Is a Fake, Iranian Police Say
2010-01-01.abstract: The police say that the video, which appears to show a police pickup truck running over a protester, was faked and that no deaths were reported in that area.

2010-01-01.title: Grand New Concert Hall Tests a City’s Frugal Ways
2010-01-01.abstract: This affluent but unassuming German city is seeking recognition with showpiece architecture, but its residents are gulping as the project’s cost soars.

2010-01-01.title: C.I.A. Takes On Bigger and Riskier Role on Front Lines
2010-01-01.abstract: The deaths of seven C.I.A. operatives in Afghanistan have highlighted the agency’s transformation to a paramilitary organization at the vanguard of America’s wars.

2010-01-01.title: Making a Little Fun of Russia’s Powerful
2010-01-01.abstract: Cartoon versions of Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin and President Dmitri A. Medvedev on a television show may be a sign that political humor is returning to Russia.

2010-01-01.title: North Korea Calls for Better U.S. Ties
2010-01-01.abstract: North Korea issued a New Year’s message that highlighted the reclusive country’s attempt to readjust the focus of six-party nuclear disarmament talks.

2010-01-02.title: Different Taliban Groups Claim Role in Afghanistan Bombing
2010-01-02.abstract: The conflicting boasts by Afghan and Pakistan militants over the suicide bombing could be used to raise money and attract recruits.

2010-01-02.title: Security Flaws in Nigeria Draw Notice
2010-01-02.abstract: The attempted Christmas Day terror attack has cast a harsh light on Nigeria’s national security agencies, revealing potentially crippling lapses.

2010-01-02.title: Serving a Father by Bringing Long-Lost Koreans Home
2010-01-02.abstract: For more than 40 years, Choi Sung-yong has devoted himself to trying to find missing South Koreans, including his father, believed to have been snatched by the North.

2010-01-02.title: Between War and Peace
2010-01-02.abstract: Darfur, Sudan's enormous western region that has become virtually synonymous with conflict, seems to be stuck between war and peace.

2010-01-02.title: Fragile Calm Holds in Darfur After Years of Death
2010-01-02.abstract: The western region of Sudan that has become virtually synonymous with conflict is “frozen” — stuck somewhere between war and peace.

2010-01-03.title: Corrections
2010-01-03.abstract: An article last Sunday about Brazil’s effort to curb illegal land seizures in the Amazon misstated the amount of land that the leader of a local Amazon residents association said he purchased 15 years ago and was subsequently forced to sell by powerful farmers. It was at least 120 acres, not 20 acres.

2010-01-03.title: Threats Led to Embassy Closings in Yemen, Officials Say
2010-01-03.abstract: The U.S. Embassy cited “ongoing threats by Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula,” the branch responsible for the failed Christmas Day bomb plot.

2010-01-03.title: Iraq Says Deal Is Near on the Last of 5 Hostages
2010-01-03.abstract: When kidnappers last week released the hostage Peter Moore an unanswered question remained: What about Alan McMenemy, one of four bodyguards captured along with Mr. Moore?

2010-01-04.title: Russia-Belarus Oil Dispute Threatens Europe’s Supply
2010-01-04.abstract: The two countries failed to renew an agreement on crude oil export tariffs that expired on New Year’s Eve, raising the prospect of another midwinter fuel shut-off.

2010-01-04.title: A Black Market Finds a Home in the Web’s Back Alleys
2010-01-04.abstract: A Cuban Craigslist allows the growing number of Cubans with access to computers and Internet connections to buy and sell with less sneaking around.

2010-01-04.title: Harsh Storm Batters Island Off the Coast of Russia
2010-01-04.abstract: Five days of blizzards and avalanches cut off air and sea links to the mainland, stranded dozens of motorists on highways, and even buried a train.

2010-01-04.title: Knut Haugland, Sailor on Kon-Tiki, Dies at 92
2010-01-04.abstract: Mr. Haugland was the last surviving member of the six-man crew that sailed on the Kon-Tiki expedition in 1947.

2010-01-04.title: Four Ugandans, Four Points of View
2010-01-04.abstract: A transgender man, an anti-gay politician, a taxi driver and a gay activist share their perspectives on the gay and transgender issues in their country.

2010-01-05.title: British Prime Minister ‘Appalled’ by Protest Plan
2010-01-05.abstract: A radical Islamic group plans to protest in Wootton Bassett, a town that has been honoring the passing hearses of British soldiers killed in Afghanistan.

2010-01-05.title: Rising Price of Faith in France’s Shrinking Parishes
2010-01-05.abstract: Villagers are having to ask hard questions about their churches as the number of parishioners dwindles and the cost of upkeep mounts.

2010-01-05.title: In Yemen, U.S. Faces Leader Who Puts Family First
2010-01-05.abstract: President Ali Abdullah Saleh is amenable to U.S. support, but he has spent more time consolidating the power of his family than battling Al Qaeda.

2010-01-05.title: In Russia, a Bankrupt Town Keeps Humming
2010-01-05.abstract: The Russian government must address the future of 300 or so “monocities,” where a single plant supplies heat, income and social security.

2010-01-05.title: The Resurgence of the Hazaras
2010-01-05.abstract: For most of Afghanistan’s history the Hazara were typically servants, cleaners, porters and little else, an ethnic Shiite minority sidelined for generations.

2010-01-06.title: Indian Official Gets Far on a Few Words
2010-01-06.abstract: Shashi Tharoor, a member of Parliament and a junior minister, has upset traditional politicians with his frequent use of Twitter.

2010-01-06.title: Kenya: Efforts to Deport Cleric Fail
2010-01-06.abstract: International airlines have refused to take a radical Jamaican-born Muslim cleric and Tanzania refused Kenya’s attempt to send him there.

2010-01-06.title: Putting Lens on Lives in Suspended Animation in Gaza
2010-01-06.abstract: A human rights group distributed video cameras to young Gazans and asked them to tell about their lives.

2010-01-06.title: Britain Tries to Ease Strain Over Bomb Suspect
2010-01-06.abstract: British officials made statements Tuesday to assuage concerns about intelligence lapses involving the man accused of trying to bomb a flight to Detroit.

2010-01-06.title: Chinese Envoy Objects to More Penalties for Iran
2010-01-06.abstract: Zhang Yesui, the Chinese ambassador to the United Nations, objected Tuesday to tougher sanctions on Iran over its nuclear program.

2010-01-07.title: Fighting Trend, China Is Luring Scientists Home
2010-01-07.abstract: China’s leaders are determined to reverse the drain of top talent that accompanied its opening to the outside world.

2010-01-07.title: Danes Study Immigrants After Cartoonist Attack
2010-01-07.abstract: In a country that already has one of the strictest immigration policies in Western Europe, the attack has spurred politicians to demand ever more stringent rules.

2010-01-07.title: Where Is the Next War Zone?
2010-01-07.abstract: It is very difficult to fight an enemy that has no single home base and attacks at random.

2010-01-07.title: At Dover, More Comfort for Mourning Families
2010-01-07.abstract: A more comfortable setting for families of the fallen to receive the remains of their loved ones.

2010-01-07.title: Suicide Bombing Puts a Rare Face on C.I.A.’s Work
2010-01-07.abstract: Since a bombing killed seven Americans at a C.I.A. base in Afghanistan, details of the lives of the victims have trickled out, providing a peek into the closed society of the spy agency.

2010-01-08.title: Former Blackwater Guards Charged With Murder
2010-01-08.abstract: Two former Blackwater guards were arrested on murder charges stemming from a shooting in Afghanistan, the Justice Department said.

2010-01-08.title: Italy Puts Swiss Haven From Taxes Under Siege
2010-01-08.abstract: Italy’s crackdown on tax evaders threatens the banking industry in a picturesque Alpine border town that profits from those who shelter their money there.

2010-01-08.title: Murky Trail for ‘Loner’ in Attack on C.I.A.
2010-01-08.abstract: Details about a bombing at a C.I.A. outpost in Afghanistan that killed seven Americans and one Jordanian intelligence officer remained shrouded in secrecy.

2010-01-08.title: Cuba: American Not a Spy, U.S. Says
2010-01-08.abstract: The American contractor arrested last month in Cuba was aiding a religious and cultural group and was not engaged in espionage activity, his employer said Thursday.

2010-01-08.title: Kashmir: Gun Battle Kills 4
2010-01-08.abstract: A gun battle prompted by a deadly militant attack on a police outpost in Indian-controlled Kashmir ended after the police killed two militants, Indian police officials said.

2010-01-09.title: Across Divide in Iraq, a Sunni Courts Shiites
2010-01-09.abstract: A Sunni Muslim politician, Sheik Hamid al-Hais is an unlikely confederate of the Iraqi National Alliance, the Shiite standard-bearer in March elections.

2010-01-09.title: Cancer Risks Debated for Type of X-Ray Scan
2010-01-09.abstract: The plan for broad use of “backscatter scanners” at airports has raised questions about the safety of delivering small doses of radiation to millions of people.

2010-01-09.title: White House Aides Said to Chafe at Slow Pace of Afghan Surge
2010-01-09.abstract: Officials say that senior advisers are frustrated by the Pentagon’s inability to live up to an initial promise to have all of the forces in the country by summer.

2010-01-09.title: U.S. Has Few Resources to Face Threats in Yemen
2010-01-09.abstract: The Obama administration’s efforts in Yemen are being constrained by a lack of in-house expertise and a fraught history with the country’s leader.

2010-01-09.title: France: Imam Deported to Egypt
2010-01-09.abstract: Ali Ibrahim el-Soudany, 36, who preached at several mosques in the troubled suburbs of Paris, was expelled to his country of origin.

2010-01-10.title: Bomb Goes Off Near Greek Parliament Building
2010-01-10.abstract: A warning call was received before the blast in Athens, which caused no injuries and little damage.

2010-01-10.title: Corrections
2010-01-10.abstract: An article last Sunday about the Afghanistan Parliament’s rejection of most Cabinet nominees submitted by President Hamid Karzai misstated the name of his choice for the minister of mines, who was confirmed. He is Wahidullah Shahrani, not Mohammed Sharwani. (There is no one named Mohammed Sharwani among the nominees.).

2010-01-10.title: Freya von Moltke, Part of a Core of Nazi Resistance, Is Dead at 98
2010-01-10.abstract: Mrs. Moltke, who disdained the title of countess, was the last living active participant in the group that opposed Hitler.

2010-01-10.title: Paradox of Plenty
2010-01-10.abstract: Africa has long been a target for plunder. Ian Fisher recounts how fortunes were built off African material riches as it remained the world’s poorest continent.

2010-01-10.title: Philippines Gun Ban Starts Ahead of Vote
2010-01-10.abstract: A nationwide gun ban took effect to stave off any increase in political violence as elections draw closer, officials said.

2010-01-11.title: Airlines Refuse to Transport Radical Cleric
2010-01-11.abstract: The radical Jamaican cleric who was deported to Gambia last week has been returned to Kenya, Kenyan news media reported late on Sunday.

2010-01-11.title: Revelers See a Dimming in a Capital’s Night Life
2010-01-11.abstract: Faced with mounting noise complaints, many Parisian bars and clubs are struggling to stay afloat, forcing a startling conclusion: Paris may soon be dead at night.

2010-01-11.title: Argentine President and Central Bank in Standoff
2010-01-11.abstract: Opposition to the president’s plan to tap foreign currency reserves led her to dismiss the central bank’s head, a move blocked by a federal judge.

2010-01-11.title: Race Riots Grip Italian Town, and Mafia Is Suspected
2010-01-11.abstract: Riots in Calabria, where organized crime is known to have a strong grip, have shone a bright light on growing racial tensions in Italy.

2010-01-11.title: Corrections
2010-01-11.abstract: An article on Thursday about Kai Eide, the departing head of the United Nations mission in Afghanistan, misstated the background of Jean-Marie Guéhenno, the former head of peacekeeping operations for the United Nations, who is one Mr. Eide’s possible successors. He is a career diplomat from France, not a general.

2010-01-12.title: As China Rises, Fears Grow on Whether Boom Can Endure
2010-01-12.abstract: A gradual flow of economic power from West to East has turned into a flood, but some wonder whether China’s rise is sustainable.

2010-01-12.title: 5 Convicted in Britain Over Protest at Parade
2010-01-12.abstract: A court found five British Muslim men guilty of using insulting language during a protest they had staged at a parade welcoming British soldiers from Afghanistan.

2010-01-12.title: Miep Gies, Protector of Anne Frank, Dies at 100
2010-01-12.abstract: Mrs. Gies was the last survivor among Anne Frank’s protectors and the woman who preserved her diary.

2010-01-12.title: Georgia: Leaders of a Mutiny Receive Prison Sentences
2010-01-12.abstract: The leaders of a brief, botched military rebellion against President Mikheil Saakashvili were sentenced to lengthy prison terms.

2010-01-12.title: Future Kenya Port Could Mar Pristine Land
2010-01-12.abstract: A sleepy resort island and Unesco World Heritage site where Muslims and bikini-clad tourists coexist may be threatened by plans for a huge seaport.

2010-01-13.title: Blair Aide Unapologetic Over Britain’s Role in Prelude to Iraq War
2010-01-13.abstract: Alistair Campbell, a close aide to former Prime Minister Tony Blair, made a defiantly unrepentant appearance before the inquiry set up to investigate Britain’s involvement in the Iraq war.

2010-01-13.title: Clinton Tries to Defuse Asian Tension
2010-01-13.abstract: Asia has emerged as a diplomatic hornet’s nest for the U.S. even beyond the perennial threat of North Korea, with strains in Japan and China.

2010-01-13.title: Ecuador: Foreign Minister Resigns
2010-01-13.abstract: Fander Falconí resigned after President Rafael Correa criticized his handling of negotiations in connection with a forest-protection project.

2010-01-13.title: Contractor Jailed in Cuba Was Aiding Religious Groups, U.S. Says
2010-01-13.abstract: Alan P. Gross, who is accused of being an American spy, is a social worker who had gone to Cuba to provide communications equipment to Jewish groups, American officials said.

2010-01-13.title: Looking Past the Facade of Italian City After Riots
2010-01-13.abstract: In Rosarno, where the worst immigrant rioting ever seen in Italy took place over the weekend, the economy is so weak that locals and immigrants are competitors.

2010-01-14.title: Islamists Press Jordan to Stop Aiding U.S. Forces in Afghanistan
2010-01-14.abstract: After a Jordanian suicide bomber blew himself up at C.I.A. base in Afghanistan, opposition groups demanded that Jordan’s government drop its pro-America stance.

2010-01-14.title: U.S. Companies Join Race on Iraqi Oil Bonanza
2010-01-14.abstract: Some of the companies have been criticized for overcharging, shoddy work and incomplete projects.

2010-01-14.title: Piercing the Sky Amid a Deflating Economy
2010-01-14.abstract: With an estimated $100 billion in debt and a burst real estate bubble, the Dubai skyline is defined not by the tallest building in the world but by a host of unfinished buildings.

2010-01-14.title: Report on Lawyer’s Death Eases a Political Crisis in Guatemala
2010-01-14.abstract: Bizarre as the story is, the conclusion of the case of a murdered journalist is likely to put an end to a political crisis that has threatened the survival of President Álvaro Colom’s government.

2010-01-14.title: France: Bill Would Outlaw Harassment by Partners
2010-01-14.abstract: The proposed law, applicable to both sexes, would cover repeated verbal abuse, threats of violence, insults and other forms of harassment.

2010-01-15.title: What’s That? 50 Degrees Isn’t Winter? Tell That to the People Shivering Here
2010-01-15.abstract: The season may be relatively mild and short in New Delhi, but it still leaves its mark.

2010-01-15.title: Toppled in Ukraine but Nearing a Comeback
2010-01-15.abstract: Viktor F. Yanukovich, a candidate known for his ties to Russia, is capitalizing on his nation’s disillusionment with its pro-Western leaders.

2010-01-15.title: Russian Attitudes Thaw on Rights Court
2010-01-15.abstract: A compromise appears to have cleared the way for Parliament’s approval of changes intended to speed up the proceedings of the European Court of Human Rights.

2010-01-15.title: Jordan: Israeli Vehicle Attacked
2010-01-15.abstract: An Israeli Embassy vehicle was the target of a roadside bomb in Jordan on Thursday, but the explosion caused no injuries, the Israeli Foreign Ministry said

2010-01-15.title: Canada: Bomb Plot Leader Apologizes
2010-01-15.abstract: A co-leader of a group that planned to bomb several targets in Ontario repeatedly apologized to Canadians and in particular to fellow Muslims at a sentencing hearing on Thursday.

2010-01-16.title: New Arrests in Japan Fund-Raising Scandal
2010-01-16.abstract: Prosecutors arrested two former aides of a leading member of Japan’s ruling party on Friday, adding to a growing scandal over campaign finance.

2010-01-16.title: On Eve of Pope’s Visit to Synagogue, Some Ask if It Will Help
2010-01-16.abstract: When Pope Benedict XVI stops in at a Rome synagogue on Sunday, the question is whether the effort will begin to repair religious tensions that have developed under his papacy.

2010-01-16.title: In Capital, Health Crisis Overwhelms Rescue Effort
2010-01-16.abstract: Port-au-Prince has become a vast refugee camp, as relief organizations struggle to give proper medical care to the thousands of injured people in the stricken city.

2010-01-16.title: Recasting Serbia’s Image, Starting With a Fresh Face
2010-01-16.abstract: Just 34, Serbia’s foreign minister isn’t afraid of challenging a popular position in the West even while his country seeks European Union membership.

2010-01-16.title: Envoys to Meet on Possible Sanctions Over Iran Nuclear Program
2010-01-16.abstract: The five permanent Security Council members, plus Germany, will try to reach agreement on new restrictions aimed at swaying Tehran.

2010-01-17.title: 3 Blackwater Guards Called Baghdad Shootings Unjustified
2010-01-17.abstract: The account of the guards, who were on the scene when the 2007 shootings occurred, was revealed in newly unsealed court documents.

2010-01-17.title: Edward Schillebeeckx, Catholic Theologian, Dies at 95
2010-01-17.abstract: Father Schillebeeckx was a prominent theologian who helped reshape Catholicism during the Second Vatican Council and whose writings were later investigated for heresy.

2010-01-17.title: 6 Major Powers Move Closer to Considering More Iran Sanctions
2010-01-17.abstract: China, however, kept to its position that it opposed new sanctions from the United Nations Security Council over Iran’s nuclear program.

2010-01-17.title: Looting Flares Where Authority Breaks Down
2010-01-17.abstract: The impulses for theft were borne of desperation and the lack of food and water as well as the absence of law.

2010-01-17.title: Haiti Quake Day 5: Hunger Frays Nerves
2010-01-17.abstract: Survivors show frustration and restraint at a locked supermarket, four days after a powerful earthquake devastated Port-au-Prince.

2010-01-18.title: Star Turns at Liberian’s War Crimes Trial
2010-01-18.abstract: The prosecution has charged that in 1997 the former warlord Charles G. Taylor gave a large rough-cut diamond to Naomi Campbell, challenging Mr. Taylor’s credibility.

2010-01-18.title: Israeli Defense Minister Visits Turkey to Mend Frayed Ties
2010-01-18.abstract: Ehud Barak traveled to Turkey on Sunday to try to smooth over recent tensions that have further frayed the traditionally strong ties between the two countries.

2010-01-18.title: Pope Tries to Soothe Tensions With Jews
2010-01-18.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI, and Jewish leaders aired one main point of contention: whether the church did enough against the Holocaust.

2010-01-18.title: Presidential Election in Ukraine Goes to a Runoff
2010-01-18.abstract: The runoff, scheduled for Feb. 7, is seen as a referendum on the Orange Revolution because it is between its two chief combatants.

2010-01-18.title: A Rescue at U.N. Headquarters, as Others Wait and Hope
2010-01-18.abstract: A Danish staff member was pulled from the rubble on Sunday, and during a visit, Secretary General Ban Ki-moon expressed sympathy for those still trapped.

2010-01-19.title: After Hearing in Pakistan, 5 Americans Allege Torture
2010-01-19.abstract: The men arrested last month in Pakistan on suspicion of plotting terrorist acts said at a court appearance on Monday that they were being tortured in custody.

2010-01-19.title: Modern-Day Pilgrims Find Interfaith Bond in Ancient Syrian Monastery
2010-01-19.abstract: An Italian priest’s passion for interfaith dialogue has brought Christians and Muslims together to a remote cliff-top sanctuary.

2010-01-19.title: U.N. Panel’s Glacier Warning Is Criticized as Exaggerated
2010-01-19.abstract: An estimate about the rapid melting of Himalayan glaciers from climate change is coming under fire as a gross exaggeration.

2010-01-19.title: Gunmen Attack a Charity in a Sunni Neighborhood of Baghdad
2010-01-19.abstract: Accounts differed on the attack in a Sunni neighborhood, in which four or five people were killed and a bomb was left for the police officers who responded.

2010-01-19.title: Guatemala: No Damage in Quake
2010-01-19.abstract: An earthquake centered just off the Pacific Coast rattled nerves and sent people into the streets, but officials in Guatemala and El Salvador said there were no reports of damage or injuries.

2010-01-20.title: Guinea Junta Picks Opponent as Premier on Path to Civilian Control
2010-01-20.abstract: Jean-Marie Doré, a veteran opposition figure, was chosen in a critical step in the transition to elections and civilian rule later in the year.

2010-01-20.title: A Sign of Latin America’s Fading Polarization
2010-01-20.abstract: The election of a right-wing billionaire to lead Chile appears to signal a convergence of left and right agendas.

2010-01-20.title: Russia Seeks to Cleanse Its Palate of U.S. Chicken
2010-01-20.abstract: The government has imposed an open-ended ban on imports of American chicken, which was given as food aid in the 1990s and became a symbol of the nation's humiliation.

2010-01-20.title: Arrests for a Revealing Dance Pit Flesh Against Faith
2010-01-20.abstract: The arrest of four women for “sexy dancing” has raised worries that it may be the prelude to the imposition of wider restrictions in Indonesia.

2010-01-20.title: After Attack, Afghans Question Motives or See Conspiracies
2010-01-20.abstract: Kabul residents seemed surprised that the insurgents who stormed a shopping center on Monday had not killed more civilians.

2010-01-21.title: U.N. Official Says Climate Deal Is at Risk
2010-01-21.abstract: Facing a Jan. 31 deadline, major countries have yet to submit their plans for reducing emissions of climate-altering gases.

2010-01-21.title: China Paints Google Issue as Not Political
2010-01-21.abstract: The Chinese government is approaching a dispute over censorship as a business conflict, not a political matter that could affect U.S. relations.

2010-01-21.title: How an Inflammatory Term, Baathist, Bars Candidates in Iraq
2010-01-21.abstract: The term “Baathist” is as malleable as it is incendiary, and the quandary it represents has underlined the growing dispute over the credibility of Iraq’s parliamentary elections.

2010-01-21.title: Accord Reached to Let Honduran President Depart
2010-01-21.abstract: The president-elect of Honduras agreed to grant safe passage to the Dominican Republic for ousted President Manuel Zelaya next week.

2010-01-21.title: Regret on Himalayan Glaciers Estimate
2010-01-21.abstract: An international scientific panel overseen by the United Nations expressed “regret” for publishing an unsupported estimate of the speed at which Himalayan glaciers were melting.

2010-01-22.title: John Burns Q. and A. on Islamic Radicalization
2010-01-22.abstract: What drives young men to embrace a violent ideology that encourages them to commit murder and kill themselves in the process? John Burns responds to “At War” readers’ questions and comments on the topic of Islamic radicalization.

2010-01-22.title: Drone Reportedly Killed Filipino in Pakistan
2010-01-22.abstract: The presence of the Filipino is another indication that Pakistan’s tribal areas have become a collecting point for militants from around the globe.

2010-01-22.title: Entrenched Monarchy Thwarts Aspirations for Modernity
2010-01-22.abstract: The financial crisis and recent criminal cases demonstrate that the United Arab Emirates remain an absolute monarchy, where the law is capricious.

2010-01-22.title: White House Eager to Project Image of Competence in Relief Efforts
2010-01-22.abstract: The Obama administration unleashed a torrent of statements and briefings after the earthquake in Haiti to document its efforts to respond quickly to the disaster.

2010-01-22.title: For Israelis, Mixed Feelings on Aid Effort
2010-01-22.abstract: Israel has sent scores of doctors and other relief to Haiti, and Israelis are watching the effort and questioning the character of their nation.

2010-01-23.title: U.S. Delays Setting Up More Anti-Taliban Militias
2010-01-23.abstract: Officials are concerned that the program of arming local forces could aid in the re-emergence of warlords in Afghanistan.

2010-01-23.title: Study Points to Disease as Main Killer in Darfur
2010-01-23.abstract: A new study assessing dozens of mortality estimates for the six years of fighting there has concluded that disease, rather than violence, killed at least 80 percent of them.

2010-01-23.title: Britain: Terrorist Threat Level Raised
2010-01-23.abstract: Britain’s intelligence services raised the threat level of a terrorist attack from “substantial” to “severe” on Friday.

2010-01-23.title: Ivory Coast: Court Rules on Payment in Toxic-Waste Dumping Scandal
2010-01-23.abstract: An appeals court ruled that compensation to some 30,000 victims of a toxic-waste dumping scandal should be turned over to a community representative.

2010-01-23.title: France: Prince to Fix Up Historic House
2010-01-23.abstract: The Qatari prince who owns the 17th-century Hôtel Lambert signed an agreement with preservationists who had opposed his plans to renovate the mansion, the Ministry of Culture said.

2010-01-24.title: More Than 150,000 Have Been Buried, Haiti Says
2010-01-24.abstract: The government provided a preliminary assessment of the quake’s body count and said the search for survivors would soon end.

2010-01-24.title: Corrections
2010-01-24.abstract: An article in some editions last Sunday about Secretary of State Hillary Clinton’s trip to Haiti on Saturday misspelled the surname of a Haitian doctor with who lives in the United States and who traveled with Mrs. Clinton. He is Herby Derenoncourt, not Derononcourt.

2010-01-24.title: Corrections
2010-01-24.abstract: An article last Sunday about the presidential election in Chile misstated the year when Gen. Augusto Pinochet, the former dictator of Chile, was arrested in Britain on a Spanish warrant that alleged he had committed crimes against humanity. It was 1998, not 1988.

2010-01-24.title: Afghanistan to Delay Parliament Vote
2010-01-24.abstract: The Independent Election Commission will announce the postponement of elections to Sept. 18, according to a Western diplomat.

2010-01-24.title: Afghanistan Postpones Parliamentary Election by 4 Months
2010-01-24.abstract: Officials said the elections would be moved to Sept. 18 because of security concerns and a financial shortfall.

2010-01-25.title: Christmas Bombing Try Is Hailed by bin Laden
2010-01-25.abstract: Osama bin Laden spoke publicly for the first time about the botched airliner bombing, praising the attempt — but not explicitly taking credit for it — in an audiotape aimed at President Obama.

2010-01-25.title: General Puts Up a Fight in Sri Lanka’s Election
2010-01-25.abstract: President Mahinda Rajapaksa is facing a challenge in Tuesday’s election from the general who led Sri Lanka’s army to victory against the Tamil Tigers.

2010-01-25.title: In Haiti, Many Amputees Have No Place to Go
2010-01-25.abstract: Health officials are facing a challenge: caring for thousands of trauma patients who are homeless or lack families to care for them.

2010-01-25.title: Pakistan’s Rebuff Over New Offensives Rankles U.S.
2010-01-25.abstract: The Pakistani Army’s reluctance to pursue militants has chipped away at one of the cornerstones of President Obama’s strategy in the region.

2010-01-25.title: Luck Trumps Death
2010-01-25.abstract: A Marine lance corporal stepped on a bomb big enough to destroy a pickup truck or kill several men, but it didn’t explode.

2010-01-26.title: The Ozone Hole Is Mending. Now for the ‘But.’
2010-01-26.abstract: The hole in the Earth’s ozone layer is slowly mending, a big victory for environmental policy makers. But a new report says its repair may contribute to global warming.

2010-01-26.title: Stitching the Narrative of a Revolution
2010-01-26.abstract: China quietly opened the archives of selected records from the Cultural Revolution, slightly lifting the veil on prosaic stories from that time.

2010-01-26.title: Fighting Starvation, Haitians Share Portions
2010-01-26.abstract: In the pulverized capital, new rules of hunger etiquette are emerging: no matter what is found, it must be shared.

2010-01-26.title: Clock Ticks for Nepal to Settle Its Future
2010-01-26.abstract: An interim Constitution expires May 28, and no one is sure what happens if a new Constitution is not approved by then.

2010-01-26.title: In Digital Combat, U.S. Finds No Easy Deterrent
2010-01-26.abstract: The U.S. is developing a strategy to deal with cyberspace attacks, a task complicated by the problem of identifying the enemy.

2010-01-27.title: Guarding an Ethnic Fault Line in Iraq
2010-01-27.abstract: American soldiers are working together with Arab and Kurdish troops at a series of new checkpoints.

2010-01-27.title: The Afghan Leader’s Hat, Always More Than Just Headgear, Is Losing Its Cachet
2010-01-27.abstract: Hamid Karzai’s karakul hat once represented Afghan unity and won admiration abroad; now its fortunes have fallen along with the president’s.

2010-01-27.title: Baking Swiss Treats Amid Acid Debate
2010-01-27.abstract: A town has benefited from a factory selling products to Muslim customers while remaining opposed to the construction of more minarets.

2010-01-27.title: Honduras: Court Clears Military Officers of Charges
2010-01-27.abstract: The Supreme Court cleared six high-ranking military officers of charges of abuse of power in last summer’s coup.

2010-01-27.title: Guatemala: Ex-President Arrested on U.S. Warrant
2010-01-27.abstract: The authorities arrested former President Alfonso Portillo on Tuesday on an extradition warrant from the United States, ending his four days on the run.

2010-01-28.title: Study Offers an Insight Into Dinosaur Colors
2010-01-28.abstract: Fossilized structures of what appear to be feathers match the feathers of living birds down to the microscopic level, and from them scientists have determined what color the ancient feathers were.

2010-01-28.title: Rule of Thumbs: Koreans Reign in Texting World
2010-01-28.abstract: Ha Mok-min and Bae Yeong-ho won an international competition to determine who could send text messages the fastest on a cellphone.

2010-01-28.title: Afghan Tribe, Vowing to Fight Taliban, to Get U.S. Aid in Return
2010-01-28.abstract: A large Pashtun tribe in a Taliban stronghold agreed to battle insurgents, and in exchange, the U.S. said it would channel $1 million to development projects.

2010-01-28.title: United Nations: New Top Envoy in Afghanistan Named
2010-01-28.abstract: Staffan de Mistura will take over March 1 from Kai Eide, a Norwegian whose tenure included a public spat over electoral fraud with his deputy, Peter Galbraith.

2010-01-28.title: Israel: Man Hits Judge With Tossed Shoe
2010-01-28.abstract: Chief Justice Dorit Beinisch was mildly hurt when a man entered the Supreme Court while it was in session and threw his shoes at her face, breaking her glasses and knocking her off her chair.

2010-01-29.title: Israel Signals Tougher Line on West Bank Protests
2010-01-29.abstract: The Israeli Army and security forces have recently begun clamping down, arresting scores of local organizers and activists and conducting nighttime raids on the homes of others.

2010-01-29.title: From Reeds to the River, a Village From Iraq’s Past
2010-01-29.abstract: In a country invaded, wrecked and not yet rebuilt, Halaichiya has the distinction of never having seen an American, much less a soldier.

2010-01-29.title: Mudslides Trap Tourists Near Peru Ruins
2010-01-29.abstract: More than 1,000 people had been evacuated by helicopter but hundreds more remained stranded near the Machu Picchu ruins, facing food and water shortages.

2010-01-29.title: Brazil: High Blood Pressure Sidelines President
2010-01-29.abstract: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva suffered an attack of high blood pressure and canceled his trip to Davos, Switzerland, for the Global Economic Forum, where he was to have accepted the Global Statesman award.

2010-01-29.title: South Korea: North Fires Into Disputed Waters Again
2010-01-29.abstract: For a second straight day, North Korea fired shells into waters near the disputed western sea border with South Korea, asserting its territorial claims in the most volatile section of the inter-Korean frontier.

2010-01-30.title: China Moves to Protect Owners of Homes Set to Be Razed
2010-01-30.abstract: A regulation would be an important shift in Beijing’s attitude toward the rights of individual property owners and would address a leading source of social unrest.

2010-01-30.title: China Insists That Its Steps on Climate Be Voluntary
2010-01-30.abstract: The Chinese set emissions goals but rejected a binding international commitment.

2010-01-30.title: Russia: Test Flight of New Fighter Is Called a Success
2010-01-30.abstract: The Russian military conducted the first successful flight of its new fifth-generation stealth fighter jet on Friday, Russian media reported.

2010-01-30.title: Giving Birth in Haiti
2010-01-30.abstract: Haitians must wait in line to give birth in a tent in the wake of the earthquake that hit the country more than two weeks ago.

2010-01-30.title: From Marines, a Softer Approach
2010-01-30.abstract: Marines have been financing local projects in an effort to build loyalty among the population.

2010-01-31.title: Security in an Insecure Land
2010-01-31.abstract: Alix Sainvil, a Haitian-American United Nations police officer, worked to secure Cité Soleil. But the disaster may cause gang violence and instability to return this poor neighborhood.

2010-01-31.title: An Iraqi in America…
2010-01-31.abstract: “Everyone here is in a rush—no time to eat, no time to talk, and, most important for me, no time for family and friends.”

2010-01-31.title: U.S. to Resume Airlift of Injured Haitians
2010-01-31.abstract: The effort was suspended five days ago after the state of Florida complained that its hospitals were overwhelmed.

2010-01-31.title: Pakistani Taliban Leader Is Reported Dead
2010-01-31.abstract: Pakistani and American officials said that they were increasingly convinced that Hakimullah Mehsud had died from wounds sustained in a drone strike.

2010-01-31.title: A Crime Writer’s Pages Come to Life in His Death
2010-01-31.abstract: In what resembled a scene ripped from the pages of one of his books, a crime writer was gunned down last month in broad daylight.
